previous_statement: (BP for conic inequalities) Let (V, V^+) have pointed cone, and for each i ∈ I let (W_i, W_i^+) be ordered and Φ_i : V → W_i linear. For C = {x ∈ V^+ : Φ_i(x) ≥_{W_i^+} a_i ∀i}, set m = Σ_i dim(W_i). If x is extreme in C and d_F is finite, then d_F ≤ m. Proof: Define Φ : V → ⊕_i W_i. If D ∈ ker Φ_F then Φ_i(D) = 0 for all i, so Φ_i(x + tD) - a_i = Φ_i(x) - a_i ∈ W_i^+ for all t. The rest follows identically to Steps 2-4 of Theorem 1 (nodes 1.3-1.4).
new_statement: (BP for conic inequalities) Let (V, V^+) have pointed cone, and for each i ∈ I let (W_i, W_i^+) be ordered and Φ_i : V → W_i linear. For C = {x ∈ V^+ : Φ_i(x) ≥_{W_i^+} a_i ∀i}, set m = Σ_i dim(W_i). If x is extreme in C and d_F is finite, then d_F ≤ m. Proof: Define Φ : V → ⊕_i W_i. If D ∈ ker Φ_F then Φ_i(D) = 0 for all i, so Φ_i(x + tD) - a_i = Φ_i(x) - a_i ∈ W_i^+ for all t. The rest follows identically to Steps 2-3 of Theorem 1 (nodes 1.3-1.4).
